name: NABL Readiness Checklist
shortBenefit: Assess lab readiness for NABL accreditation.
intro: Evaluate your hospital laboratory's preparedness for NABL (National Accreditation Board for Testing and Calibration Laboratories) accreditation across documentation, equipment, personnel and quality management.

seo.title: NABL Readiness Checklist — Lab Accreditation Assessment | Varada Nexus
seo.description: Free NABL readiness checklist. Assess your hospital laboratory across documentation, equipment, QMS and personnel requirements for accreditation.

items.text: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for all test processes
Document control system in place (version, review, distribution)
All equipment calibrated with valid certificates
Reference standards traceable to national/international standards
Equipment breakdown and repair records kept
Training records and competency assessments maintained
Authorised signatory meeting NABL educational requirements
Sample collection, handling and storage procedures defined
Uncertainty of measurement calculated for key tests
Reference ranges validated for patient population
Dedicated lab space meeting biosafety requirements
Biomedical waste management per BMWM rules
Personnel protective equipment available and used

assumptions: Checklist maps to ISO 15189:2022 / NABL Doc 112 requirements. Critical items are mandatory for initial assessment.
disclaimer: This tool provides an indicative estimate for general planning only and is not professional, legal, financial, medical or engineering advice. Reference values are editable defaults that vary by location, vendor, regulation and date. Verify critical figures with a qualified professional. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.q: What is NABL accreditation for hospitals?
faq.a: NABL (under QCI) accredits medical testing laboratories to ISO 15189, assuring patients and regulators that test results are accurate and reliable.

faq.q: How long does NABL accreditation take?
faq.a: Typically 9–18 months from readiness to certificate, covering application, pre-assessment, assessment and any corrective actions.

faq.q: What are the most common NABL audit failures?
faq.a: Missing or outdated SOPs, gaps in equipment calibration records, absence of EQA participation and incomplete uncertainty of measurement calculations.
